mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as corporate revenues accelerate.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative median 60d returns over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle confirms that real economic growth outpaces restrictive policy, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings resilience offsets higher discount rates.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns during tightening periods with a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive Fed tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as discount rates spike.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending power, mechanically boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and monthly consumption expenditure expansion confirm sustained demand momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Combined signal yields negative median returns in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of industrial production contraction alongside an unemployment rate above 5.0% signals a supply-side recessionary shock, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months while UNRATE > 5.0%
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive 60d returns in > 55% of cases.
